executiveSummary.text: US-Iran dual blockade of Strait of Hormuz enters week 3; ~1 bbl/day production lost. WTI surged 13% last week. Fed hold confirmed (3.75%), no cuts priced for 2026.
Mag 7 earnings week begins Apr 29 (MSFT, AMZN, GOOG, META) and Apr 30 (AAPL). AI infrastructure spending thesis under earnings scrutiny.
TSMC deferred ASML High-NA EUV to 2029 — capital discipline signal. TSMC capex guidance $56B in 2026 still intact.
China restricted US investment in AI/tech firms (Apr 24) — escalation of tech decoupling. Reciprocal action, not new sanctions.
Gold $4,709/oz (+0.24% Apr 24). Dollar strength partially offset Middle East safe-haven demand. Gold is real-rate-sensitive, not purely crisis-sensitive.

usGlobal.what: TSMC defers ASML High-NA EUV deployment to 2029 over cost
usGlobal.why: Capital discipline amid US fab expansion costs. $56B 2026 capex still intact. No demand collapse — TSMC prioritizing near-term capacity over leading-edge density.
usGlobal.implication: ASML near-term revenue headwind. TSM capital efficiency improves. KL's ASML/AMAT/LRCX/KLAC positions: don't panic-sell; the AI buildout capex is $56B+ and still growing.

usGlobal.what: China bans US tech investment; restricts AI firms from accepting US capital
usGlobal.why: Reciprocal action to US-China tech decoupling. Not chip sanctions but investment decoupling — broader scope.
usGlobal.implication: Long-term demand destruction risk for US semis in China. AI infrastructure thesis is US-centric; no direct impact on AI Infra positions. Monitor if expanded to equipment exports.

usGlobal.what: Iran ceasefire extended (conditional 2-week); dual blockade continues
usGlobal.why: Ceasefire fragile; US blockade of Iranian ports since Apr 13 remains in force. Hormuz effectively closed.
usGlobal.implication: Oil supply shock 2nd-order: inflation (greater than) Fed can't cut (greater than) real rates rise (greater than) energy equities under pressure vs cash. Watch if ceasefire breaks — oil spikes further.

usGlobal.what: Semiconductor index longest daily gain streak on record (AI optimism)
usGlobal.why: AI infrastructure buildout pricing. Despite geopolitical headwinds, semis outperforming. Index tracking chipmakers extended rally amid AI demand.
usGlobal.implication: Momentum extended. AI Infra thesis confirmed by market. Don't chase at all-time highs but hold core positions.

singapore.what: STI fell to 4,923 pts (-0.43%) Apr 24; recovered weekly
singapore.why: Oil price shock, rate uncertainty. But STI gained for the week of Apr 20-24 on renewed buying interest.
singapore.implication: SGBank dip buyers. High dividend yields (DBS ~5.8%, OCBC ~5.5%) provide floor. No action needed.

singapore.what: Singapore plans gold trading hub for Asia
singapore.why: Regulatory-SGX partnership to strengthen trading, clearing, storage. Targets Asia's growing gold demand.
singapore.implication: Indirect: strengthens Singapore as financial center. No direct positions.

singapore.why: March selloff overdone. AIMS APAC REIT 6.7%, CICT 4.8% (below 5.1% historical avg).
singapore.implication: REIT yields attractive vs SGS bonds. Accumulate on weakness. 2nd-order: higher oil = REIT expense inflation = DPU pressure. Keep allocation modest.

singapore.why: Chinese New Year boost. Consumer spending resilient despite macro headwinds.
singapore.implication: Positive consumer health signal. Banks benefit from loan growth.

commodities.what: Oil: WTI $96.62/bbl (+13% WoW). 1 bbl/day production lost (Vitol). Hormuz dual blockade enters week 3.
commodities.why: Largest oil supply disruption on record by daily output lost. US blockade Iranian ports since Apr 13. Ceasefire fragile.
commodities.implication: 1st order: energy stocks up. 2nd: inflation surge (greater than) Fed stuck (greater than) real rates rise (greater than) gold/REITs/equities under pressure. 3rd: consumer purchasing power crushed (greater than) discretionary spending falls (greater than) tech demand risk. KL's GRID position: energy infrastructure play.

commodities.what: Gold $4,709/oz (+0.24% Apr 24). Dollar strength partially offset safe-haven demand.
commodities.why: War (greater than) inflation expectations rise (greater than) nominal gold up BUT real rates also rise (greater than) gold opportunity cost rises. Both forces partially cancel.
commodities.implication: NO LAZY 'crisis = gold up' HEURISTIC. Real rate analysis: if Fed stays at 3.75% and inflation 4-5%, real rates negative-deepening = gold structurally supported. But dollar strength = headwind. Signal: mixed, slight bullish.

commodities.why: Silver is both precious (monetary) and industrial (solar/AI). Oil shock (greater than) industrial slowdown (greater than) silver industrial demand risk.
commodities.implication: Silver more volatile than gold. AI/solar demand thesis intact. Hold SLV for portfolio completeness.

commodities.what: Copper $6.02/lbs (-0.86% Apr 24, +8.85% MoM). Supply deficit 330K tonnes 2026.
commodities.why: Goldman Sachs: 490K surplus forecast vs supply-side reality. Demand from AI datacenters + energy transition vs macro slowdown risk.
commodities.implication: Copper is the best real-asset inflation hedge in KL's portfolio. Long-term deficit thesis strengthened. Short-term: oil shock (greater than) global growth fears (greater than) copper risk-off. Accumulate on dips.

commodities.what: Uranium $86.80/lbs (-0.40% Apr 24, +2.97% MoM, +30% YoY). Nuclear energy demand structural.
commodities.why: AI datacenters power demand + energy security + nuclear renaissance. $4.6B flowed into URA ETF 2025.
commodities.implication: Uranium structurally supported. AI power demand (greater than) nuclear + renewables the only scalable baseload answer. KL's GRID/uranium positions: hold. Cameco strong balance sheet (debt/equity 0.14).

commodities.what: NuScale SMR stock +22% late April 2026 on AI-nuclear nexus.
commodities.why: Small Modular Reactor certification + AI datacenter power demand. SMR Pioneer first US NRC certified design.
commodities.implication: SMR plays increasingly relevant as AI companies seek carbon-free reliable power. Watch GRID as broader play on AI power infrastructure.

narratives.title: Hormuz Closure: Largest Oil Supply Shock in History
narratives.description: The dual blockade (Iran + US) of the Strait of Hormuz since mid-April has caused the largest oil supply disruption on record by daily output lost. Vitol CEO: 1 billion barrels of production will be lost. Ceasefire extended but fragile; Pentagon maintains blockade for as long as it takes.
narratives.priceImpact: Oil +13% WoW; ceiling intact while blockade holds

narratives.title: Fed Trapped: No Cuts in 2026
narratives.description: Markets now pricing zero Fed cuts in 2026. War-related inflation + energy supply shock give Fed no room to ease. Powell term ends May; Kevin Warsh (Trump nominee) expected to be more dovish IF confirmed. Dual mandate conflict: employment strong but inflation fear dominates.
narratives.priceImpact: Rates unchanged; curve steepening; dollar supported

narratives.title: AI Buildout Capex: TSMC $56B, Semis Longest Rally
narratives.description: Semiconductor index longest daily gain streak ever. AI chip demand to hit $1.1T revenue 2026. TSMC raised guidance; Barclays raised TSM price target to $470. TSMC deferred ASML High-NA EUV (cost discipline, not demand failure). Overall capex remains elevated.
narratives.priceImpact: Positive momentum; earnings Apr 29-30 key catalyst

narratives.title: China-US Tech Decoupling: Investment Ban + Equipment Controls
narratives.description: China restricting US investment in AI/tech startups (Bloomberg, Apr 24). US lawmakers scaled back chip bill (removed Lam Research cryogenic etch curbs). Investment decoupling is more targeted than full equipment sanctions.
narratives.priceImpact: Bearish for China-exposed semicon equipment; AI thesis US-centric

narratives.title: Singapore: Gold Hub Ambition + STI Resilience
narratives.description: Singapore unveiled plans to become Asia gold trading hub (MAS-SGX-industry partnership). STI fell to 4,923 pts Apr 24 but weekly gain; 29% YoY return. REITs rebounded with yields 4.8-6.7%.

secondOrderEffects.text: Oil shock (greater than) energy inflation (greater than) CPI spike (greater than) Fed stuck (greater than) real rates rise. Real rates rising = gold headwind. BUT: if inflation expectations surge > nominal rate rise = real rates fall = gold tailwind. Current market pricing stuck-Fed scenario. Watch: which moves faster, inflation or nominal rates?
secondOrderEffects.implication: Gold ($4,709) is in a RANGE, not a clear directional bet. Real rate framework: hold as inflation hedge but do not expect crisis-drama up move while Fed is trapped at 3.75%.

secondOrderEffects.text: Oil shock (greater than) consumer purchasing power crushed (gas at $4+/gallon US). 2nd-order: discretionary spending falls (greater than) AI consumer services (AMZN, GOOG, META) demand risk. 3rd-order: if AI capex continues at current pace BUT consumer slows, enterprise AI may carry the economy. Earnings next week key test.
secondOrderEffects.implication: Mag 7 earnings Apr 29-30 will determine if AI spending is resilient to consumer pain. MSFT/AMZN/GOOG: watch cloud AI revenue. If guidance holds (greater than) semis rally extends. If guidance cuts (greater than) risk-off.

secondOrderEffects.text: US-Iran ceasefire extended (greater than) if it holds (greater than) oil supply returns (greater than) WTI normalizes to $70-80 range. But: geopolitical risk premium stays elevated. Iran deal unlikely under Trump admin. Long-term: Hormuz closure risk is base case for energy investors now.
secondOrderEffects.implication: Energy/GRID thesis strengthened permanently. AI infrastructure + nuclear + grid = what KL AI Infra and Metals positions capture. Uranium $86.80/lbs structurally supported by energy security premium.

secondOrderEffects.text: Fed cannot cut = dollar stays strong = EM capital outflows = Asia currencies weak = STI faces headwind. Singapore strong reserves + current account give buffer. DBS/OCBC/UOB: NIM supported by stable SGD but loan growth may slow if economy cools.
secondOrderEffects.implication: STI 4,923: no panic but expect range-bound. Accumulate Singapore blue chips on dips. REITs: yield vs bond spread still attractive at 4.8-6.7%.

secondOrderEffects.text: TSMC deferred ASML High-NA EUV to 2029. This is NOT a demand problem (greater than) it is a capital allocation decision. TSMC building US fabs (Arizona) + advanced packaging. ASML High-NA tool ($380M each) too expensive even for TSM when 2-year payback. AMAT/LRCX/KLAC: near-term unaffected; ASML carries more valuation risk.
secondOrderEffects.implication: ASML: watch for potential guidance cut from TSMC deferral. Market may overreact to 2029 deferral as demand problem when it is cost discipline. KL ASML position: hold but do not add.

investment.thesis: AI Infrastructure Capex: Semis + Equipment + Energy. Semis have longest rally streak. AI capex $1.1T 2026. TSMC $56B capex. Earnings next week key catalyst. Hold NVDA, AVGO, TSM, AMAT, LRCX, KLAC, MU, SNPS.
investment.trigger: Mag 7 earnings Apr 29-30 confirming AI guidance; SMH breakout above $260
investment.whyNow: Semis momentum extended but not exhausted. AI buildout multi-year thesis. Current pullback from ATH = opportunity.
investment.invalidator: Earnings miss; AI capex slowdown signal; China escalation restricting equipment sales

investment.thesis: Energy/Metals Barbell: GRID (AI power infrastructure) + uranium (energy security). Uranium $86.80 (+30% YoY) structural deficit. NuScale +22% shows AI-nuclear nexus re-rating. GRID: best AI infrastructure play for what AI MUST consume (electricity).
investment.trigger: Oil stabilizing at lower level; nuclear/grid bill progress; GRID breakout above $82
investment.whyNow: Hormuz crisis = energy security premium here to stay. AI cannot run on hope (needs power). Nuclear/SMR/grid is under-owned part of AI thesis.
investment.invalidator: Ceasefire holds + oil crashes (greater than) energy plays underperform; nuclear regulatory setbacks

trading.thesis: Short Oil on Ceasefire: If Iran ceasefire holds and Hormuz reopens, WTI could retrace 10-15% rapidly. Oil at $96.62 is pricing significant war premium.
trading.trigger: Verified ceasefire extension + Hormuz reopening + US Navy statement
trading.whyNow: Oil at 13-week high; ceasefire signal = asymmetric short setup. Risk/reward favorable given current premium.
trading.invalidator: Ceasefire collapses; blockade intensifies; oil spike to $110+

trading.thesis: Copper Accumulation on Dips: Copper $6.02/lbs. Goldman Sachs wrong on surplus (supply disruptions underestimated). AI datacenter copper demand + energy transition = structural deficit thesis. Buy COPX on -1% daily dips.
trading.trigger: Copper below $5.90/lbs; China stimulus announcement; AI datacenter build announcement
trading.whyNow: Copper at reasonable entry after recent +8.85% MoM. Supply deficit 330K tonnes real. Dollar weakening would be additional catalyst.
trading.invalidator: Global recession signal; China property sector collapse; new mine supply announcement

watchlist.thesis: ASML (greater than) watch for potential guidance cut from TSMC High-NA EUV deferral. Market may overreact to 2029 deferral as demand problem when it is cost discipline.
watchlist.trigger: ASML Q1 earnings (Apr 30); TSMC Q1 earnings; management commentary on High-NA EUV timeline
watchlist.whyNow: ASML most exposed to TSMC capital discipline signal. If guidance cut > 5% = entry point for long-term hold.
watchlist.invalidator: ASML confirms AI customer demand intact; High-NA EUV in 2027-28 for other customers

watchlist.thesis: EWY (Korea ETF): Kospi at all-time high 6,539 intraday Apr 23. Korea = AI semiconductor exposure (Samsung, SK Hynix). Overbought near-term but structural AI thesis. Wait for 3-5% pullback.
watchlist.trigger: Kospi pullback to 6,200-6,300; Samsung earnings; AI memory demand data
watchlist.whyNow: AI memory cycle: HBM demand strong. Samsung/SK Hynix benefiting. But Kospi extended (greater than) patience for better entry.
watchlist.invalidator: Kospi breaks 6,000 support; Samsung earnings miss; memory prices collapse

riskWatch.name: Hormuz Escalation / Oil Spike
riskWatch.description: Ceasefire fragile; dual blockade in force. If Iran closes Hormuz fully (mines, attacks), oil could spike to $110-130. 1st-order: energy up. 2nd: inflation (greater than) Fed stuck (greater than) real rates rise. 3rd: consumer crushed (greater than) global recession (greater than) equities sell off hard.
riskWatch.mitigation: GRID/energy positions hedge oil spike. Short oil on ceasefire confirmation. Reduce REITs if oil > $100 persists (expense inflation).

riskWatch.name: Fed Error / Real Rate Spike
riskWatch.description: If inflation surges (oil (greater than) gas prices (greater than) CPI) and Fed holds at 3.75%, real rates rise sharply. Negative real rates = gold/equities supported. But stagflation scenario (inflation + growth fall) = worst case for risk assets.
riskWatch.mitigation: Gold position is real-rate hedge, not crisis hedge. Cash (682k SGD = 26%) provides dry powder. Energy/commodities hedge inflation.

riskWatch.name: Mag 7 Earnings Disappointment
riskWatch.description: MSFT, AMZN, GOOG, META report Apr 29; AAPL Apr 30. AI capex guidance is key. If Mag 7 cut guidance = AI buildout doubt = semis/nasdaq correction. Consumer spending weakness from oil shock could also weigh on tech demand.
riskWatch.mitigation: AI Infra thesis is US gov + hyperscaler capex driven, not consumer. MSFT/GOOG cloud AI = enterprise, more resilient. Hold core positions; add on 10%+ pullback.

riskWatch.name: China Escalation: Tech Decoupling 2.0
riskWatch.description: China investment ban on US AI/tech firms (Apr 24). If expanded to semiconductor equipment = major headwind for AMAT/LRCX/KLAC/ASML. Currently investment decoupling, not equipment sanctions (greater than) but trajectory concerning.
riskWatch.mitigation: AI Infra thesis is US + Asia (TSMC) + Europe. China exposure limited in KL semis positions. Monitor for equipment export restrictions.

riskWatch.name: STI / Singapore Growth Slowdown
riskWatch.description: STI fell 4,923 pts. Oil shock (greater than) Singapore inflation (greater than) MAS tightens (greater than) consumer slowdown. Singapore retail sales +8.3% (Feb) is lagging indicator. STI may underperform global equities in 2026.
riskWatch.mitigation: Singapore dividend yields (DBS ~5.8%, OCBC ~5.5%) provide income floor. REITs at 4.8-6.7% yield. Accumulate on dips.

keyEvents.text: Fed rate hold (greater than) Powell likely last meeting; Kevin Warsh confirmation ahead
MSFT, AMZN, GOOG, META earnings (greater than) AI capex guidance test
AAPL earnings (greater than) iPhone cycle + services AI revenue
ASML Q1 earnings (greater than) High-NA EUV deferral reaction
WTI crude at $96.62 (+13% WoW) (greater than) Hormuz blockade week 3
China restricts US AI/tech investment (greater than) reciprocal decoupling
TSMC capex $56B 2026 intact; defers ASML High-NA EUV to 2029
Gold $4,709/oz (greater than) real rate range; not clear directional bet
Kospi hits all-time high 6,539 (greater than) Korea AI memory rally
Singapore gold hub initiative (greater than) MAS-SGX partnership

whatMatters: Hormuz blockade (greater than) oil shock is 1-in-100 year event. 1 billion barrels lost. This is the marginal price setter.
Fed cannot cut = real rates matter. Gold is a real-rate hedge, NOT a crisis hedge. Real rates rising (greater than) gold range-bound. Real rates falling (stagflation) (greater than) gold up.
Mag 7 earnings next week = AI buildout thesis validation. AI capex $1.1T 2026 is the trade. If guidance holds (greater than) semis extend rally. If cuts (greater than) 10-15% correction.
China-US tech decoupling is investment-level, not equipment-yet. Monitor for escalation. KL semis are US/TSMC/Asia-exposed, limited China exposure.
whatToDo: Hold AI Infra core positions (NVDA, AVGO, TSM, AMAT, LRCX, KLAC, MU, SNPS). Do not panic on TSMC-ASML news (capital discipline not demand failure).
GRID + uranium: accumulate. AI must have power. Nuclear renaissance + grid investment is under-owned AI thesis.
Energy/GRID provides natural oil spike hedge. GRID is portfolio energy infrastructure anchor.
Cash (26%) is a weapon. If Mag 7 earnings miss = 10%+ correction = deploy cash into semis/AI Infra at better entry.
Singapore REITs: yield attractive (4.8-6.7%). Accumulate on further STI weakness.
whatToIgnore: Daily gold headlines treating it as crisis-hedge. Gold is real-rate sensitive. Hormuz crisis is NOT necessarily bullish for gold (trace causal chain).
STI daily point movements. STI 4,923 is not a crash (greater than) it is a pause. Singapore financials are high-quality, high-yield anchors.
TSMC-ASML High-NA EUV deferral as bearish signal. It is capital allocation decision. TSMC $56B capex still intact.
Copper Goldman surplus call vs supply-disruption reality. Supply deficit 330K tonnes more credible than Goldman forecast.
